ddls:
CREATE TABLE "lisp_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Lisp file */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the Lisp file */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the Lisp file */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Keywords or tags associated with the Lisp file */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of the Lisp file */,
  "license" TEXT /* License under which the Lisp file is released */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "uploader_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_lisp_files_uploader_id FOREIGN KEY ("uploader_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, tester, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machines" (
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine */,
  "machine_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine */,
  "machine_type" TEXT /* Type of machine (e.g., CPU architecture, operating system) */,
  "machine_spec" TEXT /* Specifications of the machine (e.g., number of cores, RAM) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "file_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Lisp file */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine on which the performance metrics were collected */,
  "execution_id" INTEGER /* ID of the execution that generated the performance metrics */,
  "load_time" REAL /* Load time of the file */,
  "eval_time" REAL /* Evaluation time of the file */,
  "gc_avg_time" REAL /* Average garbage collection time */,
  "gc_min_time" REAL /* Minimum garbage collection time */,
  "gc_max_time" REAL /* Maximum garbage collection time */,
  "gc_invocations" INTEGER /* Number of garbage collection invocations */,
  "gc_least_free" INTEGER /* Least free memory */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage of the Lisp program during execution */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage of the Lisp program during execution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_execution_id FOREIGN KEY ("execution_id") REFERENCES executions ("execution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_file_id FOREIGN KEY ("file_id") REFERENCES lisp_files ("file_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "executions" (
  "execution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each execution */,
  "file_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Lisp file */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine on which the execution took place */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who executed the file */,
  "execution_date" TEXT /* Date the file was executed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the execution (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "command_line_args" TEXT /* Command-line arguments passed to the Lisp program during execution */,
  "stdout" TEXT /* Standard output of the Lisp program during execution */,
  "stderr" TEXT /* Standard error of the Lisp program during execution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("execution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_executions_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_executions_file_id FOREIGN KEY ("file_id") REFERENCES lisp_files ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_executions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
